segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是啊，我搞不太懂为什么它不像其他一些产品那样一炮而红。真可惜。

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: I can't quite work out why it hasn't been an instant success like some of the others.

segments.speaker: JAKE
segments.en: It's a shame really.

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 我知道。好了，现在我们考虑过……

segments.speaker: AMY
segments.en: OK so now we've thought about …

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One of the most famous cases of extinction is that of a bird known as the dodo.
segments.zh:
最著名的灭绝案例之一是一种叫做渡渡鸟的鸟。事实上，英语中甚至有一句谚语“as dead as the dodo”，用来指代不再存在的事物。但许多世纪以来，渡渡鸟一直活得很好，尽管它只在一个地方被发现，那就是印度洋上的毛里求斯岛。它是一种非常大的鸟，大约一米高，几个世纪以来它已经失去了飞行能力，但它快乐地生活在覆盖岛屿的树下。

然后在1507年，第一批葡萄牙船只停靠在该岛。水手们正把香料运回欧洲，发现这个岛是一个方便的停靠点，他们可以在那里为剩下的航程储备食物和水，但他们没有在毛里求斯定居。然而，在1638年，荷兰人到达并在那里建立了殖民地。这些岛上的第一批人类居民发现渡渡鸟是一种方便的食物来源，尽管不是每个人都喜欢它的味道。

很难准确描述渡渡鸟到底长什么样。我们确实有一些水手的文字记录和一些图片，但我们不知道这些有多可靠。最著名的图片是一幅荷兰画，画中的鸟看起来非常胖，但这可能不准确——同一时期的一幅印度画显示了一只瘦得多的鸟。

尽管有人试图保存一些鸟的尸体，但没有完整的标本幸存下来。在17世纪早期，已知存在四只鸟的干燥部分——其中三只已经消失，所以只有一例渡渡鸟的软组织幸存下来，即一个渡渡鸟头。骨头也被发现，但只存在一具完整的骨架。

这具唯一的渡渡鸟骨架最近成为科学研究的主题，研究表明许多早期关于渡渡鸟的信念可能是不正确的。例如，早期对鸟类的描述提到它们多么缓慢和笨拙，但科学家现在认为鸟的强壮膝关节使其能够移动，不是缓慢，而是相当快。事实上，一位17世纪的水手写道，他发现这些鸟很难捕捉。确实，渡渡鸟的小翅膀不允许它离开地面，但科学家认为这些翅膀可能用于在不平坦的地面上保持平衡。另一组科学家对渡渡鸟的头骨进行了分析。他们发现，关于渡渡鸟缺乏智力的报告并没有得到他们研究的支持，研究表明鸟的大脑并不小，而是大小适中。事实上，相对于其体型，它与鸽子相似，而鸽子被认为是高度聪明的鸟。研究人员还发现，鸟的头骨结构表明，一种特别发达的感官是嗅觉。所以渡渡鸟也可能特别擅长在岛上茂密的植被中定位成熟的水果和其他食物。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact there's even a saying in English, 'as dead as the dodo', used to refer to something which no longer exists.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But for many centuries the dodo was alive and well, although it could only be found in one place, the island of Mauritius in the Indian Ocean.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was a very large bird, about one metre tall, and over the centuries it had lost the ability to fly, but it survived happily under the trees that covered the island.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Then in the year 1507 the first Portuguese ships stopped at the island.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The sailors were carrying spices back to Europe, and found the island a convenient stopping place where they could stock up with food and water for the rest of the voyage, but they didn't settle on Mauritius.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, in 1638 the Dutch arrived and set up a colony there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These first human inhabitants of the island found the dodo birds a convenient source of meat, although not everyone liked the taste.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's hard to get an accurate description of what the dodo actually looked like.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We do have some written records from sailors, and a few pictures, but we don't know how reliable these are.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The best-known picture is a Dutch painting in which the bird appears to be extremely fat, but this may not be accurate – an Indian painting done at the same time shows a much thinner bird.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Although attempts were made to preserve the bodies of some of the birds, no complete specimen survives.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the early 17th century four dried parts of a bird were known to exist – of these, three have disappeared, so only one example of soft tissue from the dodo survives, a dodo head.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Bones have also been found, but there's only one complete skeleton in existence.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This single dodo skeleton has recently been the subject of scientific research which suggests that many of the earlier beliefs about dodos may have been incorrect.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For example, early accounts of the birds mention how slow and clumsy it was, but scientists now believe the bird's strong knee joints would have made it capable of movement which was not slow, but actually quite fast.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, one 17th century sailor wrote that he found the birds hard to catch.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's true that the dodo's small wings wouldn't have allowed it to leave the ground, but the scientists suggest that these were probably employed for balance while going over uneven ground.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another group of scientists carried out analysis of the dodo's skull.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They found that the reports of the lack of intelligence of the dodo were not borne out by their research, which suggested the bird's brain was not small, but average in size.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, in relation to its body size, it was similar to that of the pigeon, which is known to be a highly intelligent bird.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The researchers also found that the structure of the bird's skull suggested that one sense which was particularly well-developed was that of smell.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So the dodo may also have been particularly good at locating ripe fruit and other food in the island's thick vegetation.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So it looks as if the dodo was better able to survive and defend itself than was originally believed.
segments.zh:
所以看起来渡渡鸟比最初认为的更能生存和自卫。然而，在欧洲人首次到达该岛后不到200年，它们就灭绝了。那么原因是什么呢？长期以来，人们认为渡渡鸟是被猎杀灭绝的，但科学家现在认为情况比这更复杂。另一个因素可能是水手带到岛上的新物种。这些包括狗，它们会对渡渡鸟构成威胁，还有猴子，它们吃渡渡鸟主要食物来源的水果。这些是故意带到岛上的，但船只还带来了另一种生物——老鼠，它们从船上登陆并迅速占领了岛屿。这些破坏了岛屿的生态，不仅影响了渡渡鸟，也影响了其他物种。然而，它们对渡渡鸟特别危险，因为它们吃掉了鸟蛋，而每只渡渡鸟一次只下一个蛋，这可能对种群产生了毁灭性的影响。

然而，我们现在认为，鸟类灭绝的主要原因可能不是外来物种的引入，而是农业的引入。这意味着曾经覆盖整个岛屿并为渡渡鸟提供了完美家园的森林被砍伐，以便种植甘蔗等作物。所以，尽管渡渡鸟已经存活了数千年，但突然间它就消失了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Yet less than 200 years after Europeans first arrived on the island, they had become extinct.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So what was the reason for this?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For a long time, it was believed that the dodos were hunted to extinction, but scientists now believe the situation was more complicated than this.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another factor may have been the new species brought to the island by the sailors.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These included dogs, which would have been a threat to the dodos, and also monkeys, which ate the fruit that was the main part of the dodos' diet.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These were brought to the island deliberately, but the ships also brought another type of creature – rats, which came to land from the ships and rapidly overran the island.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: These upset the ecology of the island, not just the dodos but other species too.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, they were a particular danger to the dodos because they consumed their eggs, and since each dodo only laid one at a time, this probably had a devastating effect on populations.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, we now think that probably the main cause of the birds' extinction was not the introduction of non-native species, but the introduction of agriculture.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This meant that the forest that had once covered all the island, and that had provided a perfect home for the dodo, was cut down so that crops such as sugar could be grown.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So although the dodo had survived for thousands of years, suddenly it was gone.

items.prompt: Portuguese ships transporting ... stopped at the island to collect food and water.

pairs.q: Portuguese ships transporting ... stopped at the island to collect food and water
pairs.note: 题干说transporting...，原文用carrying spices，spices就是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问葡萄牙船运什么，原文说carrying spices，spices直接对应答案。

items.prompt: The Dutch established a ... on the island.

pairs.note: established 和 set up 都表示'建立'，是同义替换。
原文直接使用了 colony，与答案完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'the Dutch arrived and set up a colony'，其中 'set up' 对应题干 'established'，'colony' 对应答案。

items.prompt: A Dutch painting suggests the dodo was very ...

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全相同的词，直接定位。
very 和 extremely 是同义替换，fat 相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问荷兰画作中渡渡鸟看起来如何，定位句提到画中鸟看起来 extremely fat，对应答案 fat。

items.prompt: The only remaining soft tissue is a dried ...

pairs.p: only one example of soft tissue
pairs.note: 题干说'唯一剩下的软组织'，原文说'唯一一个软组织例子'，意思相同。
题干中的'dried'与原文中的'dried'完全一致，直接对应。
题干问软组织是什么，原文说'一个渡渡鸟头'，答案就是'head'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句在原文倒数第三段末尾：'only one example of soft tissue from the dodo survives, a dodo head'，直接对应题干中的'only remaining soft tissue'和'dried'，答案就是'head'。

items.prompt: Recent studies of a dodo skeleton suggest the birds were capable of rapid ...

pairs.p: capable of movement which was not slow, but actually quite fast
pairs.note: 题干'rapid'对应原文'fast'，通过否定'not slow'来强调快速。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到'capable of movement which was not slow, but actually quite fast'，其中'fast'与题干'rapid'同义。

items.prompt: It's thought they were able to use their small wings to maintain ...

pairs.note: 原文直接出现，帮助定位
maintain 被替换为 employed for，表示‘用于’
答案词在原文中直接出现

paraphrase.explain: 定位句为 'these were probably employed for balance while going over uneven ground'，其中 'small wings' 与题干一致，'employed for' 对应 'maintain'，'balance' 直接出现。

pairs.note: 原文直接出现 'average in size'，与题干 'average size' 几乎一致。
原文直接出现 'brain'，与题干关键词相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接提到 'the bird's brain was not small, but average in size'，其中 'brain' 和 'average in size' 与题干 'brain' 和 'average size' 对应，因此答案就是 brain。

items.prompt: Their sense of ... enabled them to find food.

pairs.note: 题干中的'sense'与原文'sense'完全一致，直接对应。
答案'smell'在原文中直接出现，与题干'sense'对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'one sense which was particularly well-developed was that of smell'，其中'smell'对应题干中的'sense'，且后文'good at locating ... food'与'enabled them to find food'同义，因此答案确定为'smell'。

items.prompt: ... also escaped onto the island and ate the birds' eggs.

pairs.p: came to land from the ships
pairs.note: 原文用'came to land from the ships'解释老鼠如何上岛，与题干'escaped onto the island'同义。
原文'consumed their eggs'与题干'ate the birds' eggs'同义，'consumed'替换'ate'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句在'rats, which came to land from the ships... consumed their eggs'，直接对应题干中老鼠上岛吃鸟蛋的描述。

items.prompt: The arrival of farming meant the ... was destroyed.

pairs.note: farming 被替换为 introduction of agriculture，意思相同。
destroyed 与 cut down 同义，都表示森林被毁。
原词重现，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问农业到来导致什么被毁，定位句提到农业引入导致森林被砍伐，因此答案 forest 直接出现在原文中。
